title: Understanding the Problem
text: The body starts with an initial velocity $$v_0$$ and is acted upon by a force that first brings it to rest and then reverses its direction to $$-v_0$$. We need to find the total distance traveled during this process.

title: Breaking Down the Motion
text:
Consider the motion in two parts:
$$1$$. From $$v_0$$ to $$0$$.
$$2$$. From $$0$$ to $$-v_0$$.
Each part takes time $$t$$.

title: Distance in Each Part
text: In the first part, the body travels a distance while decelerating from $$v_0$$ to $$0$$. In the second part, it travels the same distance while accelerating from $$0$$ to $$-v_0$$.

title: Calculating Distance for One Part
text: Use the formula for distance traveled under constant acceleration: distance $$=$$ initial velocity * time + $$0.5$$ * acceleration * $${time}^2$$. Since the velocity changes from $$v_0$$ to $$0$$ in time $$t$$, the acceleration is $$\frac{-v_0}{t}$$.

title: Total Distance Calculation
text: The total distance is the sum of the distances traveled in both parts of the motion. Since both parts cover the same distance, multiply the distance of one part by $$2$$.
